# public — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Ticket: ARCHITECT-PUBLIC-0001
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02

## Feature Overview

The public feature is the unauthenticated VPORT discovery and lead-capture surface for the VCSM platform. It is split into two full-MVC sub-modules: **vportMenu** (public menu, pricing, reviews, and QR-scan paths via slug-based routes serving any visitor) and **vportBusinessCard** (public VPORT profile, contact details, services, branding, and lead-capture form with notification dispatch). All routes are PUBLIC_ZERO_AUTH — no session is required to read or submit a lead. No engine dependencies. One cross-feature dependency: `features/notifications/adapters/notifications.adapter` consumed by the business card controller for lead owner notification dispatch.

**Source Path:** apps/VCSM/src/features/public/
**Engine Path:** None — feature-only (no engines/ imports detected)

## Active Controllers

### Sub-Module: vportMenu

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| getVportPublicDetails.controller.js | Resolves VPORT public profile/details by actorId; primary: vport.public_menu_read_model_v, fallback: vport.public_actor_seo_v | actorId presence check only — no auth required |
| getVportPublicMenu.controller.js | Fetches public menu categories and items by actorId from vport.public_menu_read_model_v | actorId presence check only — no auth required |
| getVportPublicReviews.controller.js | Loads initial review summary + first page + dimensions in parallel; also exports getVportPublicReviewsPageController for pagination | targetActorId presence check only — no auth required |
| resolveMenuSlug.controller.js | Thin pass-through: slug → actorId via resolveMenuSlugDAL (queries vport.public_menu_read_model_v) | No gate — completely open |
| resolveVportSlug.controller.js | Thin pass-through: slug → actorId via resolveVportSlugDAL (queries vport.public_actor_seo_v) | No gate — completely open |

### Sub-Module: vportBusinessCard

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| vportBusinessCard.controller.js (getVportBusinessCardPublicController) | Read: VPORT business card by slug via SECURITY DEFINER RPC read_business_card_public | Slug normalization only — no auth |
| vportBusinessCard.controller.js (submitVportBusinessCardLeadController) | Write: lead submission via submit_business_card_lead RPC; fires lead confirmation email (edge function) + owner notification | Input validation only — no auth; RPC has GRANT EXECUTE TO PUBLIC (VL-001, OPEN) |
| vportBusinessCard.controller.js (getVportBusinessCardSectionsController) | Read: business card sections by slug; derives profile_id server-side from slug (PUBLIC-002 pattern) | Slug normalization only — no auth |

**Note:** getVportPublicReviewsPageController is a secondary export of getVportPublicReviews.controller.js, not a separate file. Total controller files: 6.

## Active DALs

### Sub-Module: vportMenu

| DAL | Tables / Views | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| readVportPublicDetails.rpc.dal.js | vport.public_menu_read_model_v (primary), vport.public_actor_seo_v (fallback) | No TTL cache — double-read risk on every page mount |
| readVportPublicMenu.rpc.dal.js | vport.public_menu_read_model_v | No TTL cache — fires on every page mount alongside details DAL |
| readPublicVportReviews.dal.js | reviews.public_vport_reviews_v | Cursor-based pagination (limit+1 pattern), PAGE_SIZE=20 |
| readPublicVportReviewSummary.dal.js | reviews.public_vport_review_summary_v | TTL cache: 60s per targetActorId |
| readPublicVportReviewDimensions.dal.js | reviews.public_vport_review_dimensions_v | TTL cache: 60s per targetActorId |
| resolveMenuSlug.dal.js | vport.public_menu_read_model_v | TTL cache: 10min; only resolves VPORTs with active menu items |
| resolveVportSlug.dal.js | vport.public_actor_seo_v | TTL cache: 10min; resolves any VPORT with a profile |

### Sub-Module: vportBusinessCard

| DAL | Tables / Views | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| vportBusinessCard.read.dal.js | vport RPC: read_business_card_public | SECURITY DEFINER RPC via vportClient |
| vportBusinessCardLead.write.dal.js | vport RPC: submit_business_card_lead | Anonymous-safe SECURITY DEFINER RPC; GRANT EXECUTE TO PUBLIC (VL-001 OPEN) |
| businessCardSections.read.dal.js | vport RPC: get_business_card_sections | Accepts profileId (internal UUID) — controlled server-side from slug lookup |
| sendLeadConfirmationEmail.edge.dal.js | Supabase Edge Function: send-lead-confirmation | Fire-and-forget; anon key accepted as auth (ELEK-2026-05-27-004 OPEN) |

**Total DAL files: 11**

## Active Hooks

### Sub-Module: vportMenu

| Hook | Calls | Purpose |

| useVportPublicDetails | getVportPublicDetailsController | Loads and refreshes VPORT public profile details by actorId |
| useVportPublicMenu | getVportPublicMenuController | Loads public menu categories/items by actorId |
| useVportPublicReviews | getVportPublicReviewsController, getVportPublicReviewsPageController | Loads review summary + paginated cards + dimensions; exposes loadMore |
| useResolveMenuSlug | resolveMenuSlugController | Resolves menu slug → actorId; sets notFound on failure |
| useResolveVportSlug | resolveVportSlugController | Resolves vport slug → actorId; sets notFound on failure |
| useDesktopBreakpoint | — | Thin re-export of shared/hooks/useDesktopBreakpoint — not feature logic |

| Hook | Calls | Purpose |

| useVportBusinessCardSections | (reads from controller via index — confirmed indirect) | Loads business card sections by slug |

**Total hook files: 9** (useDesktopBreakpoint is a pass-through re-export, not feature logic)

Import follows the adapter boundary contract (via *.adapter.js). No direct internal imports from the notifications feature detected.

---

## Authorization Pattern

All routes in this feature are PUBLIC_ZERO_AUTH — no session, token, or actor identity is required to read any data or submit a lead. Authorization pattern per layer:

- **Controllers:** Input presence check only (actorId/slug not null). No actor ownership assertions. No assertActorOwnsVportActorController calls. No useIdentity() usage.
- **DALs:** All reads target public views (public_menu_read_model_v, public_actor_seo_v, public_vport_reviews_v, public_vport_review_summary_v, public_vport_review_dimensions_v). Write surfaces use SECURITY DEFINER RPCs — ownership enforcement must be handled at DB level.
- **Write path (leads):** submit_business_card_lead RPC carries GRANT EXECUTE TO PUBLIC with actor_id hardcoded to NULL (VL-001 through VL-005 OPEN). DB-level enforcement is the only gate.
- **Email path:** send-lead-confirmation edge function accepts anon key as sufficient auth (ELEK-2026-05-27-004 OPEN) — can deliver SES to arbitrary addresses.

**Critical gap:** ELEK-007/008 (confirmed RESOLVED per 2026-06-02 source inspection) — menu delete controllers had missing ownership gates; these are patched. However the submit_business_card_lead CARNAGE migration (VL-001–005) is PLAN AUTHORED but NOT EXECUTED.

---

## Module Independence Classification

**MOSTLY INDEPENDENT**

The feature has no engine imports and only one outbound cross-feature dependency (notifications adapter), which follows the adapter boundary contract. The sub-modules (vportMenu, vportBusinessCard) are internally self-contained with well-defined adapter surfaces. The primary coupling risk is the unresolved duplicate in features/profiles (VportActorMenuPublicView) which owns the /actor/:actorId/menu route — a parallel implementation with divergent data shape.

---

## Architecture State

**FLAGGED**

The overall architecture is structurally sound (full MVC layering, adapter present, cross-feature access via adapter boundary, no engine pollution), but multiple known violations are open and unresolved: (1) 4 files use relative imports instead of @/ aliases; (2) useAuth() imported inside VportPublicReviewsPanel component (auth coupling in wrong layer); (3) style objects in model layer (vportPublicMenuPanel.model.js); (4) VportPublicMenuView.jsx at 301 lines (1 over the 300-line limit); (5) duplicate route conflict with profiles feature; (6) no TTL cache on the two highest-traffic DALs (readVportPublicDetailsRpcDAL, readVportPublicMenuRpcDAL); (7) double-hook call on useVportPublicReviews causing 3 extra DB reads; (8) critical security migration (VL-001–005) not executed. Feature is THOR BLOCKED.

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. **Duplicate route conflict** — /actor/:actorId/menu is owned by features/profiles/kinds/vport (VportActorMenuPublicView), not features/public. Exposes raw UUID in back navigation, divergent hook data shape `{ loading, details, error }` vs canonical `{ result, details, loading, error, refresh, rpcErrorCode }`, and has console.log violations. No consolidation ticket exists.

2. **No TTL cache on high-traffic read DALs** — readVportPublicDetailsRpcDAL and readVportPublicMenuRpcDAL both hit vport.public_menu_read_model_v on every public page mount. resolveMenuSlugDAL adds a 3rd hit (cached 10min after first call). For a QR-scan-primary surface this is every visitor's first paint with 3 sequential DB reads.

3. **Double hook on useVportPublicReviews** — When Reviews tab is active, useVportPublicReviews is called twice: once in VportPublicMenuView for header rating, once in VportPublicReviewsPanel internally. Results in 3 redundant DB reads (summary + page + dimensions) on every tab open.

4. **Relative import violations** — 4 files use relative `../` imports instead of @/ aliases:
   - vportMenu/controller/resolveMenuSlug.controller.js
   - vportMenu/controller/resolveVportSlug.controller.js
   - vportMenu/hooks/useResolveMenuSlug.js
   - vportMenu/hooks/useResolveVportSlug.js

5. **Auth coupling in component layer** — useAuth() imported inside VportPublicReviewsPanel component; should be a prop passed from the view layer.

6. **Style objects in model layer** — PANEL_STYLE and THUMB_WRAP_STYLE defined in vportPublicMenuPanel.model.js. Model layer must not contain style objects.

7. **VportPublicMenuView.jsx at 301 lines** — 1 line over the 300-line architectural limit.

8. **Dead write-review CTA** — VportPublicReviewsPanel CTA redirects to /login; no review submission screen is wired post-auth. Feature is publicly broken.

9. **Five DB views untracked by CARNAGE** — public_menu_read_model_v, public_actor_seo_v, public_vport_reviews_v, public_vport_review_summary_v, public_vport_review_dimensions_v have no migration history entries.

10. **Critical security migration unexecuted** — VL-001 through VL-005 (submit_business_card_lead: GRANT EXECUTE TO PUBLIC, actor_id = NULL, permissive INSERT, full-row UPDATE grant, missing source CHECK constraint). PLAN AUTHORED, NOT EXECUTED.

11. **Wildcard CORS open** — ELEK-2026-05-27-001: all 5 edge functions including the public lead submit path carry wildcard CORS. No patch applied.

---

## Recommended Handoffs

- **CARNAGE** — Execute VL-001 through VL-005 migration plan (submit_business_card_lead RPC hardening). Also track and version the 5 untracked DB views. This is the gating action before THOR can clear.
- **ELEKTRA** — Scoped re-run: edge functions (send-lead-confirmation, submit_business_card_lead post-migration), wildcard CORS (ELEK-2026-05-27-001), lead submission path end-to-end.
- **SPIDER-MAN** — First test coverage run for this feature: fromPublicRow() UUID exclusion, DAL ownership predicates, lead submit validation boundary.
- **KRAVEN** — Double-read issue on vport.public_menu_read_model_v (3 hits per page mount); double-hook on useVportPublicReviews (3 redundant DB reads per tab).
- **VENOM** — Full second pass now that ELEK-007/008 are patched; scope should include wildcard CORS and edge function auth hardening.
- **IRONMAN** — Formal ownership audit to resolve split ownership conflict with profiles feature on /actor/:actorId/menu route.

---

## Final Module Status

**MOSTLY COMPLETE**

All layers are present, the adapter boundary is defined, cross-feature imports follow contract, and the feature serves production traffic. Architecture is classified FLAGGED due to unexecuted critical security migration (VL-001–005), open performance risks, 4 relative import violations, layer violations (auth coupling in component, style in model), a duplicate route conflict in the profiles feature, and zero test coverage. THOR is BLOCKED.
